skill.name: baoyu-markdown-to-html
skill.description: Convert Markdown files into styled HTML for WeChat Official Account and similar publishing workflows, with themes, code highlighting, frontmatter, diagrams, footnotes, alerts, and image handling.
skill.summary: Converts Markdown into styled, WeChat-ready HTML with themes, code highlighting, diagrams, and publishing helpers.
skill.author: jimliu

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static critical heuristic as an aggregation of legitimate renderer behavior, regex .exec() calls, SVG numeric paths, and template literals. Confirmed risks remain: the skill runs npx bun, reads and writes local files, performs outbound network requests for media and diagrams, and emits HTML that is not safe for untrusted Markdown. No evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: External Renderer Execution
medium_findings.description: The converter invokes npx with bun to run the bundled renderer. The command is not shell-concatenated, which lowers injection risk, but it still executes external tooling and may download or run package-managed code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct use of spawnSync is confirmed, and arguments are array-based rather than shell-expanded. The risk is real but appears tied to the intended conversion workflow.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Network Access During Rendering
medium_findings.description: The skill can download remote images from Markdown and can fetch PlantUML SVG output from the default public PlantUML server. This can leak requested URLs or document diagram content to external hosts and can be abused for unwanted outbound requests.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network behavior is directly implemented and depends partly on Markdown-provided image URLs or diagram content. The behavior is legitimate for rendering but should be disclosed.

medium_findings.title: Generated HTML Is Not Sanitized for Untrusted Markdown
medium_findings.description: Several renderers interpolate Markdown-derived values into HTML attributes or element bodies. This is acceptable for trusted publishing input but creates XSS or markup injection risk when processing untrusted Markdown.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple HTML interpolation sites are visible, but the tool is a renderer rather than a sanitizer. The risk depends on whether input Markdown is trusted.

low_findings.title: Environment-Controlled Theme File Reads
low_findings.description: The renderer reads theme configuration and theme CSS from environment-controlled paths. This can broaden filesystem access if the environment is controlled by an attacker, but no exfiltration path was found beyond generated output.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variables and file reads are confirmed. Impact is limited because the paths are used for theme discovery and CSS loading inside the local conversion process.

low_findings.title: Regex exec False Positives
low_findings.description: Many static hits labeled Python exec or process exec are JavaScript RegExp.exec calls used by Markdown tokenizers. They do not execute code and are false positives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited calls are regular expression parsing calls on Markdown source text. They are not process execution or dynamic code evaluation.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Obfuscation False Positives
low_findings.description: The md5 usage is a non-security cache filename hash for remote image URLs, and the PlantUML/base64/character-code logic implements documented diagram encoding. No credential hashing, evasion, or malicious obfuscation evidence was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code context shows cache naming and PlantUML URL encoding. These patterns are not used to protect secrets or conceal malicious payloads.

dangerous_patterns.title: spawnSync External Command
dangerous_patterns.description: Runs an external npx bun renderer as part of normal conversion. This pattern requires trust in the local toolchain and package resolution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command call is explicit and reachable during normal conversion.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Fetch and Download Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: Downloads remote images and fetches PlantUML SVG content. These paths can make outbound requests based on document content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Outbound request code is explicit and tied to Markdown media and diagram rendering.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTML DOM Insertion
dangerous_patterns.description: Uses innerHTML and outerHTML in browser-side rendering helpers. This should only process trusted renderer output and reviewed Markdown content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: DOM insertion APIs are confirmed. The immediate values are renderer-controlled in some paths, but untrusted Markdown can influence surrounding generated content.

content.value_statement: Publishing Markdown to WeChat often requires inline styles, compatible HTML, and careful media handling. This skill converts local Markdown files into themed HTML with publishing-focused features.
actual_capabilities: Converts a local Markdown file into an HTML file in the same directory.
Applies default, grace, or simple themes with inline CSS for publishing compatibility.
Reads frontmatter for title, author, description, and summary metadata.
Supports code highlighting, tables, alerts, footnotes, ruby text, Mermaid, PlantUML, and infographics.
Downloads remote Markdown images to temporary files and replaces placeholders with local image metadata.
Backs up an existing HTML output file before writing a new version.
limitations: Requires Node tooling and runs an npx bun rendering command during conversion.
Remote images, PlantUML diagrams, and dynamic language loading can contact external services.
Generated HTML is not a security sanitizer for untrusted Markdown content.
Theme support is limited to bundled themes unless external theme paths are configured.

use_cases.title: Prepare WeChat Articles
use_cases.description: Convert a finished Markdown draft into styled HTML that can be pasted into a publishing workflow.

use_cases.title: Publish Technical Notes
use_cases.description: Render code blocks, diagrams, footnotes, and tables into a polished article format.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Documents
use_cases.description: Apply consistent themes and metadata handling to recurring Markdown publishing tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Conversion
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one local file
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert my Markdown file at docs/article.md to styled HTML using the default theme.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Theme
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert posts/product-update.md to HTML with the grace theme and report the output path.

prompt_templates.title: Keep the First Heading
prompt_templates.scenario: Preserve the title inside the article body
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert notes/tutorial.md to HTML with the simple theme and keep the first heading in the content.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Publishing Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert launch-plan.md to WeChat-ready HTML, preserve diagrams and remote images, and tell me if an existing output file was backed up.

output_examples.input: A Markdown article with frontmatter, headings, images, and code blocks.
output_examples.output: A same-directory HTML file with themed layout, inline styles, highlighted code, and extracted article metadata.

output_examples.input: A technical note containing PlantUML, Mermaid, tables, and footnotes.
output_examples.output: A styled HTML document where supported diagrams, tables, references, and code sections are rendered for publishing.

output_examples.input: An article path where the matching HTML file already exists.
output_examples.output: A new HTML file plus a timestamped backup of the previous output file.

best_practices: Review remote image links before conversion because the renderer may download them.
Use trusted Markdown sources when publishing the generated HTML.
Confirm the theme before conversion to avoid unwanted formatting changes.
anti_patterns: Do not run this on untrusted Markdown that may contain active HTML or unsafe links.
Do not expect it to validate diagram content or external image hosts.
Do not use it as a general HTML sanitizer or security filter.

faq.question: What input does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses a local Markdown file path and converts that file into HTML.

faq.question: Where is the output saved?
faq.answer: The HTML file is saved in the same directory as the input Markdown file.

faq.question: Which themes are available?
faq.answer: The bundled themes are default, grace, and simple.

faq.question: Does it support code blocks?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports syntax highlighting for common languages and can dynamically load additional highlighting grammars.

faq.question: Can it render diagrams?
faq.answer: It supports Mermaid, PlantUML, and infographic blocks when the needed runtime dependencies and services are available.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted Markdown?
faq.answer: No. It generates HTML for publishing and should be used with trusted or reviewed Markdown content.
